seo_title: Current Time in Monrovia — Greenwich Mean Time (GMT, UTC+0)
seo_description: Current time in Monrovia, Liberia. Live GMT clock — UTC+0 year-round, no Daylight Saving Time. Monrovia time vs London, New York, Lagos, Dakar.

faq.question: What time is it in Monrovia right now?
faq.answer: Monrovia, the capital of Liberia, operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT/UTC+0) year-round. Liberia does not observe Daylight Saving Time, meaning UTC+0 is a fixed permanent offset. Monrovia shares its exact time with Dakar, Senegal, Abidjan, Ivory Coast, and Reykjavik, Iceland — all permanent UTC+0 cities. In winter, Monrovia is also on the same time as London. Use the live clock above for the exact current time.

faq.question: What time zone is Monrovia in?
faq.answer: Monrovia is in the Greenwich Mean Time zone (GMT/UTC+0), the baseline of the world's timezone system. Liberia uses a permanent UTC+0 offset with no seasonal adjustments. While London also uses GMT as its standard time (UTC+0 in winter), London advances to BST (UTC+1) in summer — leaving Monrovia 1 hour behind London from late March to late October. The IANA timezone identifier for Liberia is Africa/Monrovia.

faq.question: Does Monrovia observe Daylight Saving Time?
faq.answer: No. Liberia does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Monrovia maintains UTC+0 throughout the year. This means Monrovia shares its clock with London only during GMT months (approximately late October to late March). When London switches to British Summer Time (BST/UTC+1) in summer, Monrovia remains at UTC+0, placing it 1 hour behind the UK capital for about 6 months of the year.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Monrovia and London?
faq.answer: Monrovia and London are on the same time when London observes GMT (UTC+0) in winter, from late October to late March. When London advances to British Summer Time (BST/UTC+1) from late March to late October, Monrovia is 1 hour behind London. When it is 12:00 PM in London (BST), it is 11:00 AM in Monrovia. This seasonal shift is the only variable in the Monrovia-London time relationship.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Monrovia and New York?
faq.answer: Monrovia is 5 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time (EST/UTC-5) in winter, and 4 hours ahead when New York switches to Eastern Daylight Time (EDT/UTC-4) in summer. When it is 9:00 AM in New York (EST), it is 2:00 PM in Monrovia. The gap narrows to 4 hours during US summer months, creating a slightly better afternoon overlap window for transatlantic communications.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Monrovia and Paris or Berlin?
faq.answer: Monrovia is 1 hour behind Paris and Berlin when those cities are on Central European Time (CET/UTC+1) in winter. When France and Germany switch to CEST (UTC+2) in summer, Monrovia becomes 2 hours behind. When it is 3:00 PM in Paris (CET) during winter, it is 2:00 PM in Monrovia. The morning hours in Europe — roughly 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM CET — align with Monrovia's 8:00 AM to 12:00 PM window, providing a workable collaboration corridor.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Monrovia?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Monrovia run 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM GMT Monday through Friday. Government ministries and public institutions often operate 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Banks are generally open 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM on weekdays. The informal market sector in central Monrovia — including Waterside Market and the Red Light Market district — operates from early morning to early evening, typically 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM daily including weekends. The port of Monrovia, one of West Africa's deep-water ports, operates around the clock.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Monrovia from Europe?
faq.answer: From London in winter (GMT/UTC+0), Monrovia is on the same time — call during any standard UK working hours to reach Monrovia simultaneously. In summer (BST/UTC+1), London is 1 hour ahead of Monrovia — call 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM BST to reach Monrovia from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. From Paris or Berlin (CET/UTC+1 in winter), Monrovia is 1 hour behind — call 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM CET to align with the 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM Monrovia window. In summer (CEST/UTC+2), call 11:00 AM to 7:00 PM Central European time.

content_blocks.title: Monrovia Time Zone Guide — Permanent GMT (UTC+0)
content_blocks.content: Monrovia, the capital of Liberia, operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT), the prime meridian offset of UTC+0, fixed permanently with no Daylight Saving Time. Located at 6.3°N, 10.8°W on the West African Atlantic coast, Liberia adopted UTC+0 in 1972 after a brief historical period using Monrovia Mean Time (a non-standard -0:44:30 offset that persisted through the colonial era). Since 1972, Monrovia has been on GMT year-round, placing it alongside Accra (Ghana), Dakar (Senegal), Abidjan (Ivory Coast), and Reykjavik (Iceland) as permanent UTC+0 cities. Unlike London, which advances to BST (UTC+1) in summer, Monrovia never adjusts. This makes Monrovia a rare case of a West African capital that is sometimes behind London (during British Summer Time) and sometimes at the same offset. The IANA timezone identifier is Africa/Monrovia. Liberia's economy, driven by rubber (home to the Firestone Plantation, one of the world's largest), iron ore mining, timber, and a growing maritime registry (Liberia's ship registry is one of the world's largest by tonnage), connects it primarily to European, Asian, and North American trading partners.

content_blocks.title: Liberia's Economy and International Connectivity
content_blocks.content: Liberia's strategic position on the West African Atlantic seaboard and its GMT timezone give it a natural morning overlap with European business centers. When London, Paris, and Frankfurt open at 8:00–9:00 AM local time in winter, Monrovia is simultaneously at 8:00–9:00 AM (GMT) or 7:00–8:00 AM (when Europe is ahead in summer). The Liberian maritime registry — operated by the Liberian International Ship and Corporate Registry (LISCR) — is a major industry, with thousands of vessels registered globally and managed by offices in New York, Geneva, Tokyo, and other hubs. The simultaneous morning overlap between Monrovia and these offices facilitates real-time communication during key business hours. Liberia also hosts significant operations by international organizations including USAID, the UN, and the World Food Programme. The country's post-civil war reconstruction period (roughly 2003 onwards) has attracted substantial foreign investment in mining (particularly iron ore from the Nimba Range), rubber, and agricultural concessions. Roberts International Airport, located outside Monrovia, handles international connections primarily to Brussels, Accra, and other African hubs.

content_blocks.title: Calling Monrovia Internationally — Best Windows by Region
content_blocks.content: Monrovia operates on GMT (UTC+0) year-round, making time calculations straightforward. From London in winter (GMT/UTC+0), Monrovia is at the exact same time — call during any standard UK working hours (9:00 AM to 5:00 PM) to reach Monrovia simultaneously. In summer (BST/UTC+1), London is 1 hour ahead — call 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM BST to reach Monrovia during its 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM window. From Paris or Berlin (CET/UTC+1 in winter), Monrovia is 1 hour behind — call 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM Central European Time. From New York (EST/UTC-5 in winter), Monrovia is 5 hours ahead — a New York caller must reach out between 4:00 AM and 12:00 PM Eastern to catch the full Monrovia workday. In summer (EDT/UTC-4), the gap narrows to 4 hours: call 5:00 AM to 1:00 PM Eastern. From Los Angeles (PST/UTC-8), Monrovia is 8 hours ahead, making real-time calls to Monrovia's business hours achievable only from 1:00 AM to 9:00 AM Pacific — strongly favoring asynchronous communication. From Toronto, the same schedule as New York applies. Dubai (GST/UTC+4) is 4 hours ahead of Monrovia — a Dubai caller can reach Monrovia between 12:00 PM and 9:00 PM Gulf time. Liberia's country code is +231.
